data.title: Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Q4 Loss Of US$19.9 Million Tests Profitability Narrative
data.description:
Intuitive Machines (LUNR) closed FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$44.8 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.17, alongside a net loss of US$19.9 million, while on a trailing 12 month basis revenue was US$210.1 million and EPS stood at a loss of US$0.71 with a net loss of US$83.9 million. Over recent quarters, the company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$62.5 million in Q1 2025 to US$50.3 million in Q2, US$52.4 million in Q3 and US$44.8 million in Q4, with EPS losses ranging between US$0.06 and US$0.22 across the period. Taken together, the latest print keeps the story centered on scaling revenue while working to tighten loss-making margins.

See our full analysis for Intuitive Machines.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the dominant bull and bear narratives around Intuitive Machines and where those narratives might need a reset.

See what the community is saying about Intuitive Machines

Advertisement

TTM losses of US$83.9 million keep profitability in focus

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Intuitive Machines booked US$210.1 million in revenue against a net loss of US$83.9 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.71, which sits alongside five year losses that have grown at about 53.5% per year.
  • Critics highlight in the bearish narrative that persistent negative margins and heavy dependence on large government contracts could keep earnings under pressure, and the current loss profile lines up with that concern as quarterly net loss moved between US$7 million and US$25.3 million through FY 2025 while still relying heavily on mission and program awards to convert backlog into revenue.
    • The move from a Q4 2024 net loss of US$149.3 million to a Q4 2025 net loss of US$19.9 million shows that recent period results differ markedly from earlier TTM loss levels, which both bulls and bears will likely factor into their views on how quickly margins might improve.
    • Bears also point to the capital intensive model and customer concentration risk, and the trailing 12 month loss of US$83.9 million alongside ongoing investments highlighted in the narratives gives that risk a clear numerical anchor for anyone watching the path to profitability.
On this view, skeptics may feel recent earnings still leave little room for error before contract delays or cost overruns start to bite into already negative margins, and they may look to how management talks about funding and contract diversification in future updates before getting more comfortable with the long term earnings path.🐻 Intuitive Machines Bear Case

High growth forecasts vs current P/S of 10.7x

  • Forecasts in the data call for revenue growth of about 29.2% per year and earnings growth of about 55.77% per year with profitability expected within three years, yet the stock is currently trading on a P/S of 10.7x compared with peer and industry averages of 5.2x and 4.6x.
  • Supporters in the bullish narrative argue that leadership in lunar missions and deep space infrastructure plus vertical integration could justify paying this higher multiple, but the current P/S premium means those growth expectations already carry a price tag that investors need to be comfortable underwriting.
    • The same narrative talks about revenue potentially reaching around US$519.5 million and earnings of US$39.4 million by 2028, which is a large step up from the trailing 12 month revenue base of US$210.1 million and loss of US$83.9 million, so any slowdown versus the projected 30% plus growth path could leave the P/S ratio exposed.
    • Analysts in the bullish cohort also assume margins move from roughly negative triple digit levels today to positive mid single digit or better in a few years, so the current valuation effectively leans on a shift from loss making to profitable that has not yet appeared in the historical EPS trend of quarterly losses between US$0.06 and US$0.22 per share in FY 2025.
If you are weighing the bullish case, it really comes down to whether those growth and margin targets justify paying roughly double the peer and industry P/S while the company is still in loss making territory today.🐂 Intuitive Machines Bull Case

Volatile share price alongside a US$49.46 DCF fair value

  • The stock trades at US$18.91 against a DCF fair value in the data of US$49.46, implying a large modeled gap, at the same time as the risk summary flags high share price volatility in the past three months plus dilution and insider selling over the past year.
  • Consensus narrative views this kind of set up as a trade off between a potential valuation gap and execution plus sentiment risks, and the combination of a modeled fair value well above the current price with a P/S multiple already ahead of peers shows how sharply opinions can split.
    • On one side, the roughly 61.8% discount to the DCF fair value and forecast revenue growth of about 29.2% per year appeal to investors who put more weight on long term contract potential and expected profitability within three years than on recent volatility.
    • On the other side, the same data highlights shareholder dilution and recent insider selling, which some investors treat as warning signs that can add pressure in a stock that is already described as highly volatile and still posting an annual loss of US$83.9 million on US$210.1 million of revenue.

data.title: China Resources Cement Holdings SEHK 1313 Margin Rebound Challenges Long Term Bearish Narratives
data.description:
China Resources Building Materials Technology Holdings (SEHK:1313) has put fresh numbers on the table for FY 2025, with Q4 revenue of C¥5.99b and basic EPS of C¥0.022 helping to lift trailing twelve month EPS to C¥0.069 on revenue of C¥21.05b. Over recent quarters the company has seen quarterly revenue move between C¥4.63b and C¥5.99b, while basic EPS has ranged from C¥0.003 to C¥0.029. This gives investors a clearer view of how earnings are tracking through the year. With net profit margins higher than a year ago, the latest results keep the focus firmly on how efficiently the business is turning that revenue base into bottom line performance.

See our full analysis for China Resources Building Materials Technology Holdings.

With the headline figures in place, the next step is to set these results against the most common market narratives to see which storylines hold up and which ones get challenged by the data.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

TTM net margin lifts to 2.3%

  • Over the last 12 months, net profit margin sits at 2.3%, compared with 0.9% a year earlier, on trailing revenue of C¥21,054.8m and net income of C¥479.4m, so more of each yuan of sales is reaching the bottom line than in the prior year.
  • What stands out for a bullish view that focuses on improving profitability is that this higher 2.3% margin comes alongside a relatively steady trailing revenue base between roughly C¥21.1b and C¥23.3b. This
    • supports the idea that margin efficiency rather than rapid top line expansion is doing more of the work behind the C¥0.069 trailing EPS,
    • yet also leaves room for questions about how far earnings can go if forecast revenue growth of about 4% per year remains below the wider Hong Kong market’s 8.3% forecast.
To see how this margin picture fits into the wider story on growth, risks, and valuation, it helps to look at how other investors are framing the company in community narratives, not just the recent numbers alone.Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

EPS rebound set against weak 5 year trend

  • Trailing EPS of C¥0.069 follows a 127.3% earnings increase over the past year, yet the five year history shows earnings shrinking by about 62.6% per year, so the recent rebound sits on top of a much softer longer term track record.
  • Bears who focus on that five year decline get some backing from the numbers, because
    • quarterly EPS has swung from a loss of C¥0.01399 in Q4 2024 to C¥0.021694 in Q4 2025, and between C¥0.003 and C¥0.028598 within FY 2025, highlighting that profitability has not been smooth over time,
    • while the longer trend of earnings falling at about 62.6% per year limits how much weight some investors may put on a single year of recovery, even with the stronger trailing result.

Premium 20.9x P/E with DCF upside signal

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 20.9x, above peer and industry averages of about 14.6x and 14.4x. A DCF fair value of HK$3.28 sits well above the current price of HK$1.63, which shows the stock trading at roughly half of that modelled value.
  • What is interesting for a bullish narrative that leans on valuation is that
    • the forecast 27.34% annual earnings growth over the next three years is higher than the Hong Kong market’s 11.8% forecast, which some investors may see as one reason for the premium 20.9x multiple,
    • but at the same time, with revenue only expected to grow about 4% per year, the case that the HK$3.28 DCF fair value is justified rests heavily on earnings and margin assumptions rather than strong top line expansion.

data.title: Tilman Fertitta Bid Puts Caesars Entertainment Value And Future In Focus
data.description:
• Tilman Fertitta is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire Caesars Entertainment, ahead of a rival bid from investor Carl Icahn.
  • The potential deal would combine Fertitta's Golden Nugget casino interests and sports assets with Caesars Entertainment's Las Vegas Strip footprint.
  • This development is attracting attention from shareholders as a possible turning point for NasdaqGS:CZR.

Caesars Entertainment, trading as NasdaqGS:CZR, last closed at $27.44. The share price return is up 44.8% over the past 30 days and 16.5% year to date, while the value score is currently 5. Over longer periods, returns have been mixed, including a 2.9% decline over the past year and deeper pullbacks over 3 and 5 years.

The emergence of Tilman Fertitta and Carl Icahn as competing bidders is drawing renewed attention to how Caesars Entertainment might evolve from here. Any change of control could affect priorities for its casino portfolio, balance sheet focus, and capital allocation. For existing and potential shareholders, a key consideration is how any final deal terms might compare with the current $27.44 share price and recent share price performance.

The reported discussions between Tilman Fertitta and Caesars Entertainment put the business model under a spotlight. A potential US$34 per share takeout level, versus the recent US$27.44 close, effectively forces the market to ask what Caesars is worth as an operator of Las Vegas Strip resorts, regional casinos and a growing digital arm. Fertitta controls Golden Nugget casinos, an NBA franchise and a large hospitality portfolio, so an eventual deal could reshape how Caesars competes with peers such as MGM Resorts and Wynn Resorts, particularly on cross marketing, loyalty programs and event driven traffic.

  • A change of control could alter priorities for capital spending, debt reduction and digital investment, which may challenge assumptions about how management executes on current plans.
  • The potential for asset combinations with Golden Nugget properties or sports holdings is not explicitly covered in the narrative and could introduce new revenue mix and cost structure variables.

data.title: Is ASML Holding (NasdaqGS:ASML) Pricing In Too Much After Its 87.5% One Year Surge
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether ASML Holding's current share price really reflects what you are getting for your money, this article will keep the focus squarely on what you are paying versus what you may be getting in return.
  • The stock last closed at US$1,366.39, with returns of 1.1% over 7 days, a 3.8% decline over 30 days, 17.4% year to date and 87.5% over 1 year, giving plenty of recent price action for valuation-minded investors to weigh up.
  • Recent coverage has continued to highlight ASML Holding's role in the semiconductor supply chain and its position in advanced chipmaking tools. This keeps the stock firmly on many investors' watchlists and helps explain why the shares can see sharp moves as sentiment shifts around demand for high-end manufacturing equipment.
  • Right now, ASML Holding has a valuation score of 1 out of 6. The rest of this article will unpack what that means using common valuation approaches and then finish by looking at a broader way to think about value that goes beyond any single model.

ASML Holding scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: ASML Holding Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today.

For ASML Holding, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, starting from last twelve months free cash flow of about €10.48b. Analyst style projections and extrapolations from Simply Wall St extend this out for ten years, with free cash flow estimates such as €8.33b in 2026 and €19.87b in 2030. After 2030, the projections continue using estimated growth rates to build a longer term cash flow stream in euros.

Those projected cash flows are discounted back and combined to give an estimated intrinsic value of €771.59 per share. Compared with the recent US$1,366.39 share price, this DCF output implies the stock is about 77.1% above the modelled valuation, so the market price is well ahead of this particular cash flow based estimate.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests ASML Holding may be overvalued by 77.1%. Discover 49 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for ASML Holding.

Approach 2: ASML Holding Price vs Earnings

For a profitable business, the P/E ratio is a useful way to gauge how much you are paying for each unit of earnings, which is often easier to relate to than cash flow models. Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower, more conservative multiple.

ASML Holding currently trades on a P/E of 46.85x. This sits above the semiconductor industry average P/E of about 39.82x, yet below the peer group average of 53.03x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 40.25x for ASML Holding. This is an estimate of what the P/E could be given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks, rather than just a simple comparison with peers.

Because the Fair Ratio is below the current 46.85x P/E, and is tailored to ASML Holding’s own profile instead of relying only on broad industry or peer numbers, this framework points to the shares trading above that Fair Ratio estimate.

Result: OVERVALUED
